summary: SKFontManager.MatchTypeface returns null for custom fonts loaded via SKTypeface.FromStream() in Blazor WASM because custom-loaded fonts are not registered with the system font manager, and the method was deprecated for not working on all platforms.

stepsToReproduce: Load custom font via SKTypeface.FromStream() from embedded resource
Result is null instead of a matched typeface

bugSignals.errorMessage: SKFontManager.Default.MatchTypeface returns null for custom-loaded typeface

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: SKFontManager.MatchTypeface was removed entirely in 3.x (PR #2539). The method was marked [Obsolete] because it did not work on all platforms.

analysis.summary: SKFontManager.MatchTypeface was an [Obsolete] API that searched system fonts for a typeface matching an existing one's family with a new style. Custom fonts loaded via FromStream() are not registered with the system font manager, so MatchTypeface cannot find them. On WASM/Blazor, the system font manager has no fonts at all, making this always return null. The method was deprecated in 2018 and removed in 3.x.
analysis.rationale: The reporter expects MatchTypeface to work with custom-loaded fonts, but the method only searches system-installed fonts. This is by-design behavior of Skia's font manager. The method was already marked [Obsolete] with the note 'Matching fonts from an existing typeface will be going away and it is not working on all platforms' (commit 6814514). It was fully removed in PR #2539. On WASM, there are no system fonts, guaranteeing null. This is close-as-by-design rather than a code defect.

keySignals.interpretation: Custom font not in system font manager collection — MatchTypeface only searches system fonts.

keySignals.text: Based on docs, it should never return null
keySignals.interpretation: The 2.88 docs were misleading; MatchTypeface can return null when no match is found, especially for non-system fonts.
WASM environment has no system fonts — font manager is effectively empty.

codeInvestigation.finding: MatchFamily searches system fonts by family name and style via sk_fontmgr_match_family_style. There is no MatchTypeface method in current code — it was removed. The font manager only knows about system-installed fonts, not custom-loaded ones.
SKTypeface.FromFamilyName searches system fonts. SKTypeface.FromStream/FromFile/FromData load fonts independently of the font manager — they are not registered with any font manager.
SKFontStyleSet.CreateTypeface(SKFontStyle) can match a style within a font family's style set, but only for fonts known to the font manager.

workarounds: Load each font style variant (Regular, Bold, Italic) as separate font files using SKTypeface.FromStream() or SKTypeface.FromData()
Use SKTypeface.FromFamilyName(familyName, SKFontStyle.Bold) if the font is installed as a system font (not applicable for WASM custom fonts)
nextQuestions: Does the reporter have separate Bold/Italic font files available, or only a single font file?

resolution.hypothesis: MatchTypeface only searches system-installed fonts. Custom fonts loaded via FromStream are not added to any font manager. On WASM there are no system fonts at all. The method was deprecated for this exact reason.

proposals.title: Load each style variant separately
proposals.description: Instead of trying to match a typeface style through the font manager, load each style variant (Regular, Bold, Italic, BoldItalic) from separate embedded font files.

proposals.title: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x and use direct loading
proposals.description: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x where MatchTypeface is removed entirely. Use SKTypeface.FromStream/FromData for each style variant. The 3.x API makes the correct pattern clearer.

resolution.recommendedReason: The simplest approach that works on all platforms including WASM. No dependency on system font manager.

actionability.reason: MatchTypeface was deprecated because it doesn't work on all platforms. Custom fonts loaded via FromStream are not registered with the system font manager, so MatchTypeface cannot find them. This is expected Skia behavior, not a SkiaSharp defect.

actions.description: Apply bug, SkiaSharp, and WASM labels
Explain by-design behavior and provide workaround
Link to related issue about MatchTypeface segfault on Linux
Link to related issue about creating typeface with different style
Close as by-design — deprecated API that was removed in 3.x
